basis: Anchor 30 plan lists Muldrotha as a BGU graveyard permanent recursion Commander anchor.
Public Commander sources label Muldrotha as a Commander deck commander.
cEDH examples exist but the main ManaLoom value is resilient casual/high-power recursion.

source_limit_notes: Aggregate/manual reference only; no copied public decklist.
Prioritize permanents because Muldrotha replays one permanent of each type per turn.
Oracle/Hermit Druid lines are high-power signals, not default casual behavior.

themes.name: graveyard_permanent_recursion
themes.notes: Self-mill plus replayable permanents gives Muldrotha resilient card advantage.

themes.name: self_mill_value
themes.notes: The graveyard functions as a second hand when the deck has enough permanent types.

themes.name: replayable_control_pieces
themes.notes: Seals, utility lands, and sacrifice permanents become repeatable interaction.

examples: generic Sultai spells with few permanents
avoid_patterns.reason: Muldrotha cannot replay instants and sorceries from the graveyard.

examples: no answers to Rest in Peace or Bojuka Bog
avoid_patterns.reason: The deck needs anti-hate tools and a backup plan.
Red is outside Muldrotha color identity.
